item.dc:creator: keeshi

item.title: A curious dilemma
item.description:
(Did I do this right?? Red is rad??) Anyway, this is happening. Over the last few years, the relative has developed pretty severe neurological disorder, similar to Conversion Syndrome. Basically, his body is so overwhelmed with stress that he’s having seizures. He has lost the ability to work or care for himself. It’s sad and scary. For him. Politics aside, I am a healthcare professional who literally specializes in trauma-informed nervous system rehabilitation, among other things. My family knows this, theoretically. I could probably help him. If anyone asked me.
Until they do, however, I’ll be keeping my mouth shut in between sips of scotch.
(And so of course Im expected to feel very sorry for this individual, as if they are the unfortunate innocent victim of whatever vast government conspiracy etc yada yada yada)
Anyway. Just venting a bit. I really do hope they get better, but I really can’t find it in me to feel sorry for them. We live in interesting times, friends. Do your best, and let that be enough. Viral Edit: Well, I had kind of expected this to die in usersub, but here we are! Thanks to everyone for the encouragement, suggestions, and the laughs. My favorite scotch at the moment is called The Deacon - it has a plague doctor on the bottle and tastes like smoked moss. For those of you curious about what I do: yes, trauma and stress can absolutely move into your body and cause real physical problems, especially autoimmune. It’s not in your head, and you’re not alone! Talk therapy is always valuable, but body-based activities like restorative yoga and EFT can make a huge impact all by themselves, as can meditation. Google “heart rate variability” and consider investing in wearable tech to help you monitor it. Set healthy boundaries and give yourself permission to prioritize your health, and don’t accept a physician telling you “there’s nothing to be done.”
item.dc:creator: vikingladywizard

item.title: It's another unhinged XL Friday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
greetings, online friends
that looks really good rn tbh
thank you, toad king
she must be the breadwinner
i can relate to this because i'm a hardened criminal
dare i say, unhinged
you heard correctly
mmm hmm
relatable
sort of
Terry has shown us so much
yep
always
ok
nice
yes
that's all for now.. have a great weekend gang!!
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

item.title: I made a really beautiful breakfast and I just wanted to share.
item.description: Been going through some pretty dark stuff recently. But for some blessed reason I got plenty of sleep last night and woke up early and motivated to make an awesome breakfast for the family. It felt really nice to feel normal again.

item.title: People don't realize what is happening with the sun right now. So I made this post to help!
item.description: Our Sun is going through the end of its eleven year solar cycle. Right now, it has a bad case of bronchitis, basically. There are enormous coronal mass ejections, and coronal holes in the sun. They're spewing high radiation particles at Earth, which is causing all kinds of issues with communication and wireless internet, as well as Auroras. The Sun is MASSIVE. The size of these "holes" are 60 Earths across. So I made this image to give a sense of how massive they are. You can read more here. https://www.space.com/sun-coronal-hole-earth-auroras-dec-2023 And if you'd like to track the Aurora to see if you can see it where you live, you can use this website here. https://www.swpc.noaa.gov/products/aurora-30-minute-forecast
item.dc:creator: MakoRuu

item.title: Another monumental fuck up.
item.description: Wanted to make some ramen noodles for lunch, turned on the wrong burner. The cooking dish that was on that burner exploded when I moved it off, and all the hot glass melted the floor in my friend's rental apartment. Oh, for context I'm staying with them after a suicide attempt, because I have nowhere else to go and I guess I haven't quite punished them enough for their kindness. Edit: Front Page with a whole lot of people hell bent on misunderstanding the frustration felt. It was never a question of my friends being mad at me, or not forgiving me for my mistake. It was always the additional burden levied onto them, a growing list that I have no real feasible way to repay. Please stop sending me messages of empty platitudes, there are no magic words that can make up for the profound sickness of our species on this world, and no small graces or kindnesses that make up for the evil and greed.
item.dc:creator: Meladyx

item.title: The Idiots of Imgur: A Mini Dump pt 138
item.dc:creator: UncleSlam123

item.title: Return of the Meme: 1049 (raven cowboy)
item.description:
Belated morning! I slept through my alarm, and Pippin let me sleep in as well, but now I am awake and everyone's problem. Let's get this bread.
(I hope so)
Pippin, this morning (halfway through the night, I put him in his cage next to the bed so I could attempt sleeping on my side)
Heyyyyyo
Yeah, what the hell, Monitor Lizards?
The Great American Faunal Interchange happened when North and South America connected. Many species in South America became extinct, while others thrived and moved into North America.
This is a King Eider.
Moopsy on the Poopsy.
I wish I heard this more often.
We lost the Spectacled Cormorant at around the same time as the Steller's Sea Cow.
Replace the raven mask with a Scrub-Jay mask and... yeah, that would *probably* be my fursona.
I choose Ponderosa Warbler and Cascadia Warbler for these guys.
*Western Roman Empire.
Yeah... not to mention the Airbnb "industry" has basically made it so that people local to this area can't afford to rent a house. To the point that Sedona plans on "solving" the problem with a parking lot for workers to sleep in. I fucking hate late stage capitalism.
Yup.
Yuuuuuup
Heeeeyyyyyyyyyyyo
Pippin tax! Today's message from Pippin: *kissing noise*
item.dc:creator: CodyBurkett

item.title: Cute birb.
item.description: Thx to user @Oddlegs83 we now know the source give em some love!: https://www.instagram.com/lochlomondbirdofpreycentre/ "Right I'm fairly sure this is at the loch lomond birds of prey centre and they deserve lots of credit. This bird was given to them as a last chance before it was put to sleep. It was so unmanageable and so many people had tried and failed to look after it. But the llbopc work their magic and saved the bird. It now takes part in shows and is very entertaining to watch. The bird does so much weird stuff in its enclosure that there is a sign up to tell visitors that the bird is ok that it's not in distress. He lies on his back, chases shadows and rolls around. But in the shows he does so much cool stuff."
item.dc:creator: NuddelNinja

item.dc:creator: pikajane

item.title: Imgur asked me to share something on my cake day, so I share this!
item.description:
I made this upside down apple pumpkin cake for Thanksgiving!
Ingredients 1 Granny Smith 1 Honeycrisp About 2.5 oz Butter 3/4 cup Brown sugar 3 Tbsp cold tap water 1 tsp vanilla 1 tsp fine sea salt (I used Diamond baking salt) 2 cups all purpose flour, I used Gold Medal Blue 1 1/2 cups granulated sugar 1 tsp baking soda 2 tsp baking powder 1 Tbsp pumpkin pie spice mix 1/2 tsp fine sea salt 3 large eggs, room temp 1 cup vegetable oil 1 can or 15 oz of Libby’s pure pumpkin Recipe -preheat your oven to 350F -peel, core and cut your apples into wedges (each piece should be about an 1/8th size) -whisk together the dry ingredients: flour, sugar, baking soda and powder, pumpkin pie spice and salt in a large bowl -now in a separate bowl, whisk your wet ingredients: eggs, oil, and pumpkin -in a medium saucepan, add the water and brown sugar together on medium heat to melt the sugar. Bring to simmer, add the butter and continuously whisk (be quick here so as to not let the pan overflow) until the caramel reduces and it will coat the back of a spoon. Add salt and vanilla to finish. -take the pan off heat and toss your apples in to coat them, then place the apples at the bottom of a round cake pan until the surface is covered (be sure not to overcrowd it though) -pour the remains of the saucepan over the apples -whisk your cake’s wet and dry ingredients together and then pour over the apples -bake for about 35-45 minutes or until a toothpick poked in the center comes out clean. You can also temp the center of the cake, goal is 200-210F. Another technique is to gently press the center and see if it bounces back. If you have left an indentation, it’s not ready.
item.dc:creator: cupidsmoke
